title: Report links Starliner problems to overconfidence and unrealistic schedules
summary: A report attributes Boeing's CST-100 Starliner technical issues to overconfidence, unrealistic schedules, and NASA's limited oversight. The findings highlight systemic challenges in the spacecraft's development.

title: FCC to vote on satellite licensing overhaul July 22
summary: The FCC plans to vote on July 22 to overhaul its satellite licensing process, aiming to streamline applications for large and complex satellite constellations. The proposed changes aim to create a more efficient 'licensing assembly line' to handle the growing demand.

title: Unseen threats overhead: Drones endanger U.S. rocket launch sites
summary: Drones pose a significant risk to U.S. rocket launch sites, potentially causing millions in delays and affecting multiple launch providers. The issue highlights growing concerns over airspace security in the space industry.

title: Vantor offers up-to-date imagery with WorldView 3D
summary: Vantor, formerly Maxar Intelligence, introduced WorldView 3D on July 1 to deliver high-definition and up-to-date imagery to customers. The new offering aims to enhance the currency of satellite imagery.

title: EchoStar’s satellite TV and wireless subsidiaries file for bankruptcy
summary: EchoStar's subsidiaries in satellite TV and abandoned 5G network businesses filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. The move follows a prepackaged restructuring plan to repay debt early after selling spectrum to SpaceX and AT&T.

title: Latitude plans to conduct first launch from Oman
summary: French startup Latitude plans to conduct its first launch of a small launch vehicle from a spaceport in Oman in late 2027. This marks a significant milestone for the company's commercial space ambitions.

title: Blue Origin outlines new launch pad approach as it pushes to return New Glenn to flight
summary: Blue Origin plans to return its New Glenn rocket to flight by the end of the year after a recent pad explosion. The company is rebuilding its launch pad with a new approach to resume operations.

title: The SpaceX IPO tells one story. Here is the more important one.
summary: The article discusses the significance of SpaceX's IPO beyond just the company's rockets, valuation, and personalities, highlighting its broader implications for the space industry.

title: NASA awards nearly $600 million in lunar lander missions
summary: NASA has selected three companies to conduct four lunar lander missions worth nearly $600 million as part of its lunar base plans. The agency is also considering launching a spare Mars rover.

title: TechnoMile Recognized among Notable Vendors in Contract Lifecycle Management Platforms Landscape Report
summary: TechnoMile has been recognized as a notable vendor in Forrester's Contract Lifecycle Management Platforms Landscape Report for Q2 2026. The company offers an AI-driven solution that integrates growth, contracts, compliance, and security workflows.

title: Watch Atlas V rocket launch 29 Amazon Leo broadband satellites early on July 2
summary: A United Launch Alliance Atlas V rocket will launch 29 Amazon LEO broadband satellites from Florida on July 2. The event can be watched live.

title: 'Rocket's Red Glare': How NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission celebrated America's 250th birthday
summary: NASA celebrated America's 250th birthday by painting 'America 250' on the Artemis 2 rocket. The mission involved launching astronauts around the moon.

title: This weird 'hot Jupiter' exoplanet has a hotspot in the wrong place, and astronomers aren't sure how
summary: Astronomers discovered a peculiar hot Jupiter exoplanet with a misplaced hotspot, defying current theories. The unusual finding raises questions about the atmospheric dynamics of these extreme planets.

title: The growing number of satellites in orbit could soon make telescopes obsolete. 'For astronomy, this would obviously be catastrophic'
summary: The increasing number of satellites in orbit threatens to make ground-based telescopes obsolete, potentially hindering humanity's ability to study the universe. Some companies plan to deploy millions of satellites, exacerbating the issue.

title: July's planetary lineup is changing — and Venus is the last one standing
summary: Venus remains the only prominent planet in the evening sky this July, while Saturn, Mars, and Uranus are visible to early risers. The planetary lineup is shifting, with Venus standing out as the last visible evening planet.

title: NASA will send a soccer ball to the moon —if the US wins the World Cup
summary: NASA will send a soccer ball to the moon if the United States wins the FIFA World Cup. The announcement was made by agency chief Jared Isaacman on June 30.

title: Ocean-monitoring satellite spots wildfire smoke from space | Space photo of the day for July 1, 2026
summary: A NASA satellite designed for ocean monitoring has unexpectedly captured images of wildfire smoke from space. This discovery highlights an additional application of the satellite's observational capabilities.

title: Stunning new NASA space telescope images reveal the universe in red, white and blue for America 250
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory released four stunning images of cosmic wonders in red, white, and blue to celebrate America's 250th anniversary on July 4. The images showcase various celestial phenomena in vibrant colors.

title: In 1776, the solar system only had 6 planets. Now, it has 8. Does it end there?
summary: The news reflects on the solar system's evolution from 6 to 8 planets and questions if this marks the end of discoveries. It ties this to the U.S. bicentennial celebration.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar landers as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. This marks a significant step toward establishing a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent New Glenn anomaly that caused significant damage. The company is working to restore the facility after the incident.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Several orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to perform an air launch for the Swift Boost Mission to extend the lifespan of a NASA space telescope. This marks the rocket's first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, providing data to support future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The findings highlight Euclid's capabilities in studying galactic structures.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX completed a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate visitors about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's orbital launch manifest includes six launches worldwide, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. This test marks a significant step in the development of SpaceX's reentry technology.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy month of operations aboard the station.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The news source 'TEC Business to Business website' is mentioned, but no specific space industry news is provided in the input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events aim to inform industry about upcoming activities, priorities, and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm developed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential impact on future missions. This innovation is expected to enhance robotic operations in space exploration and satellite servicing.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials to ensure spacecraft survive space conditions. The team includes many young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust and reliable space technology, likely related to spacecraft or satellite components designed for harsh conditions.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge has revealed plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The approach aims to minimize expenses by utilizing locally sourced materials for support structures and shielding.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA commended Blue Origin's progress in addressing past mishaps and awarded contracts for moon base missions. The agency aims for monthly lunar landings using updated lander designs.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions, focusing on lunar landers and their role in upcoming Moon missions. It highlights the importance of these missions for future lunar exploration and science.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. This collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satellite communications market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion for the company into the space applications sector.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from Esrange Space Center in Sweden by 2028 under a new franchise model. This marks Firefly's first European launch site partnership.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: ISS Risk Margin ‘Alarming’ As NASA Nears 2030 Extension Date
summary: The ISS faces increasing operational risks as NASA approaches its 2030 extension deadline, with the risk margin for regular operations described as 'alarmingly' low. A NASA advisory committee chair highlighted the urgency of addressing these concerns.

title: SpaceX Alum Have Raised $9.2B, New Data Shows
summary: Former SpaceX employees have collectively raised $9.2 billion for their new ventures, highlighting SpaceX's role as a talent incubator. The data underscores the company's impact on fostering innovation and entrepreneurship in the space industry.

title: Firefly Acquires Autonomous Space Ops Company
summary: Firefly Aerospace acquired Space-ng, an AI-powered vision navigation and autonomous guidance systems provider. This acquisition aims to enhance Firefly's autonomous space operations capabilities.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the applications dominating satellite internet traffic and their significance beyond government use. It highlights the growing importance of non-governmental satellite networks as thousands of new satellites are launched.

title: Dish Satellite TV and Wireless Businesses File for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: Dish Network's satellite TV and wireless businesses filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy to facilitate the transition of its Dish Wireless operations after spectrum transactions. The move aims to restructure debt and streamline operations amid financial challenges.

title: Ovzon Wins New Defense Deal with European NATO Nation
summary: Ovzon secured a $7.1 million defense contract with a European NATO nation for its satellite communications solution. The deal is valued at 74 million Swedish krona.

title: Unseenlabs Orders 2 Cubesats from GomSpace
summary: Unseenlabs has ordered two additional cubesats from GomSpace for 2.2 million euros ($2.5 million). The contract was announced by GomSpace on Wednesday.

title: Lite Coms Hires Calspan Exec Greg Campbell as President
summary: Lite Coms has appointed Greg Campbell, a former Calspan executive, as its new president. Campbell will lead the company's daily operations and focus on expanding manufacturing capabilities and investments.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines have secured NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of efforts to establish a lunar base. The missions are part of NASA's broader initiative to support sustainable lunar exploration.

title: C-Band Round 2: FCC Set to Vote on Another C-Band Auction
summary: The FCC is planning to vote in July on whether to approve auctioning 160 megahertz of the upper C-band, following the first C-band clearing. This move aims to further allocate spectrum for wireless services.

title: Thales Alenia Space Wins Software-Defined Satellite Order from Es’hailSat
summary: Thales Alenia Space has secured a software-defined satellite contract from Es’hailSat for the Eshail-3/Türksat-Biruni satellite, which will provide high-speed services. This deal marks a significant collaboration between the two companies in the Middle Eastern satellite market.

title: New Megaconstellation Startup Eclipse Space Targets Sovereign Market
summary: Eclipse Space, a new megaconstellation startup founded by SpaceX alumni, has emerged from stealth mode to target sovereign governments and enterprises for constellation ownership. The company aims to make satellite constellation ownership accessible to sovereign markets.

title: Saudia and Neo Space Group Linefit Inaugural Airbus A321XLR Aircraft With IFC
summary: Saudia and Neo Space Group have equipped the first Airbus A321XLR aircraft with NSG Skywaves in-flight connectivity (IFC). This marks the inaugural line-fit activation of the technology aboard Saudia's new aircraft.
